task_completion.description: The OpenBCI GUI application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenBCI GUI window is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The specific Motor Imagery dataset file is loaded into the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The playback control panel or system control shows 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' as the active loaded file.

task_completion.description: The Band Power widget is opened in the GUI layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget panel titled 'Band Power' is visible within the OpenBCI GUI interface.

task_completion.description: The Band Power widget is configured to show data for Channel 3 and Channel 4 only.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Band Power widget, only the bars/data for Channel 3 and Channel 4 are visible or active. Other channels (1, 2, 5-8, etc.) must be toggled off or hidden in this specific widget.

task_completion.description: The data stream is active and has been running for at least 5 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The playback timeline or time series widget shows that the data is actively streaming and the current timestamp is at or beyond 0:05.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenBCI GUI interface being clicked and manipulated, rather than terminal commands being used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply open an existing image or mock the final screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the step-by-step process of opening the GUI, loading the file, and configuring the widgets, not an image viewer displaying a pre-made screenshot.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely saved the screenshot to the requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool, terminal command (like scrot/import), or GUI save dialog is used to capture and save the screen to '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/mu_rhythm_config.png'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file to load is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt'. The 'Band Power' widget must be visible and configured to display ONLY Channels 3 and 4 (which correspond to C3 and C4). The stream must be active and past the 5-second mark. The final screenshot must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/mu_rhythm_config.png'.
